publicUrl.label: Public URL

tailscale.title: Tailscale

tailscale.notAvailable: Tailscale is not available on this machine.
tailscale.copyCommand: Run this command manually if needed

privateDatabase.title: Private database access
privateDatabase.description: Connect this instance to your Tailscale network and access Postgres from trusted devices without opening port 5432 to the internet.
privateDatabase.securityNote: Almirant creates a Tailscale sidecar and a private TCP proxy to Postgres. The database is not exposed on the VPS public interface.
privateDatabase.provisioningDescription: Almirant is connecting the Tailscale sidecar and proxy. Inputs are locked until the job finishes.
privateDatabase.connectedSummary: Private database access is active. Configuration is locked to avoid accidental changes.
privateDatabase.authKeyHint: Fast setup with a one-off or reusable auth key.
privateDatabase.oauthClientHint: Recommended for long-lived managed access with scoped tags.
privateDatabase.editHelp: To modify this connection, paste a fresh Tailscale auth key or OAuth secret. Existing secrets are never shown again.

capacity.title: Deployment & Capacity
capacity.description: Host facts, runner memory budget and safe concurrency guardrails for this self-hosted instance.
capacity.errorDescription: Almirant could not load host capacity data. Check the backend logs and runner heartbeat.

metrics.availableRamHint: Current headroom before runners and upgrades compete.
metrics.cpuCoresHint: Used to bound safe parallel agent work.

guardrails.title: Safe capacity guardrails
guardrails.description: These limits are derived from RAM, CPU and upgrade headroom. They are not arbitrary form values.

recommendedEnv.title: Recommended production values
recommendedEnv.description: Use these values in the managed install/reconfigure flow or in .env.production before recreating the runner.
recommendedEnv.hint: The UI is intentionally diagnostic first; it does not mutate .env.production directly.

messages.no_runner_heartbeat: No online runner heartbeat is available; capacity is estimated from the backend container.
messages.ram_budget_disabled: Runner RAM budget is disabled; concurrency is only protected by the slot cap.
messages.reserved_below_recommendation: Reserved RAM is below the recommendation for this host.
messages.configured_concurrency_above_safe_max: Configured concurrency exceeds the safe maximum for this host.
messages.insufficient_runner_budget: The reserved memory leaves less than one default agent job worth of RAM for runners.
messages.low_upgrade_headroom: Current available RAM is low for running agents while building frontend/backend images during an upgrade.
messages.unknown: Review the capacity configuration before increasing concurrency.

workers.title: Runner heartbeat
workers.description: Online runners plus offline runners that still have actionable orphaned jobs.
workers.empty: No runner has reported heartbeat data yet.
workers.hiddenOffline: {count} historical offline runner(s) hidden because they have no active jobs attached.

orphanedJobs.title: Orphaned jobs
orphanedJobs.description: Active jobs assigned to offline or missing runners. Cancelling them releases AI state and prevents stuck work.

upgrade.title: Upgrade readiness
upgrade.description: Current available headroom is {headroom}. Pause or drain agent work before upgrades when warnings are present, because frontend/backend image builds need this memory too.

operations.title: Operations & recovery
operations.description: Safe operational controls to recover runners and allowlisted services without SSH access.
operations.errorDescription: Check the backend and updater sidecar before running actions.
operations.updaterUnavailableDescription: Restart actions require the updater sidecar to be online. The UI never talks to Docker directly.
operations.servicesDescription: Only allowlisted services are shown. There is no arbitrary shell or direct Docker access from the browser.
operations.runnerActiveWarning: The runner has {count} active job(s). Restarting it can leave orphan containers.

confirm.restartService: You are about to restart {service}. Continue?
confirm.forceRunnerRestart: The runner has active jobs. Restarting it can leave orphan containers. Force restart anyway?
confirm.cleanupExited: This will remove agent containers that are already stopped. Running containers are not touched. Continue?

integration.scriptTagDescription: Add the widget to any website with a simple script tag. No build step required.
integration.reactHookDescription: Use the React hook for full programmatic control over the widget.
integration.reactComponentDescription: Drop the component into your layout for a zero-config integration.

feedbackWidget.title: Send Feedback
feedbackWidget.description: Help us improve by sharing your thoughts
feedbackWidget.messagePlaceholder: Tell us what's on your mind...

hero.title: You have more agents than clarity.
hero.subtitle: You already use AI to write code. But you're still the bottleneck, prompting, waiting, reviewing, re-explaining. Almirant separates the thinking from the executing, so your agents actually ship while you focus on what matters.

sessionSlave.title: You can't walk away
sessionSlave.description: Prompt, wait, review, fix, repeat. You started the evening with a clear goal. Three hours later, you're still in the terminal, babysitting an agent that keeps almost getting it right. AI was supposed to free your time. Instead, it chained you to a new kind of work.

blindSupervision.title: You have no idea what's happening
blindSupervision.description: Three agents running. One might be done. One might be stuck. One might be rewriting your auth module. You won't know until you check each terminal, read each diff, and piece it all together. This isn't supervision. This is archaeology.

amnesia.title: Monday morning, it's all gone
amnesia.description: You open a new session. The architecture decisions from Friday? Gone. The pattern you established? Forgotten. You spend 30 minutes re-explaining your own project to an AI with the memory of a goldfish. Every. Single. Session.

audience.title: Is this for you?
audience.subtitle: Almirant is built for developers who already use AI agents and want to stop being the bottleneck.

soloBuilder.title: Solo builder
soloBuilder.description: You build alone. You use Claude, Cursor, or Codex daily. You manage your projects with Notion, Linear, or nothing. Your day is prompt, wait, review, repeat.
soloBuilder.value: Almirant lets you plan once and walk away while agents execute in parallel.

freelancer.title: Freelancer / Contractor
freelancer.description: You juggle multiple client projects. Every context switch between projects and tools costs energy. You need your agents to work without you watching.
freelancer.value: Almirant keeps persistent context per project, so agents pick up where they left off.

smallTeam.title: Small team / Agency
smallTeam.description: 2-5 people, multiple client projects. Coordination between humans was already hard. With agents in the mix, it's invisible.
smallTeam.value: Almirant gives your team a shared dashboard to supervise agent work across all projects.

features.title: What changes when agents actually know the plan
features.subtitle: Each phase is built to solve a specific problem you've already felt.
features.shift: What if you could do the thinking, decide what to build, shape the plan, make the calls, and then walk away? Come back to pull requests, not prompts.

ideation.title: Ideas that survive first contact with reality
ideation.description: AI-assisted brainstorming that doesn't just agree with you. Challenge scope, identify risks, and validate assumptions before committing resources.
ideation.bullets1: Structured brainstorming with AI that pushes back on weak assumptions
ideation.bullets2: Automatic risk and dependency identification before you start building
ideation.bullets3: From vague idea to validated scope in one session

planning.title: Plans agents can actually follow
planning.description: The gap between your intent and the agent's output is usually a planning problem. Almirant generates structured plans with enough detail that agents don't guess.
planning.bullets1: Auto-generated epics, stories, and tasks with definitions of done
planning.bullets2: Dependency mapping so agents don't step on each other
planning.bullets3: Priority-aware sequencing across your entire project

execution.title: Parallel, not sequential
execution.description: Stop doing one thing at a time. Distribute tasks across multiple agents, each matched to the right complexity level, all running simultaneously.
execution.bullets1: Multiple agents working in parallel with dependency awareness
execution.bullets2: Right model for the right task, not everything needs your most expensive API
execution.bullets3: Queue management that respects task dependencies and priorities

supervision.title: Control without the terminal
supervision.description: Know what's happening without checking six terminals. A dashboard that shows status, progress, and where your attention is actually needed.
supervision.bullets1: Real-time agent status and task progress
supervision.bullets2: Cost tracking per task, sprint, and project
supervision.bullets3: Step in on blocked tasks, review completed PRs, on your schedule

integrations.title: Works With Your Stack
integrations.subtitle: Multi-model, multi-tool, zero lock-in. We don't compete with your AI tools. We make them work together.

claude.name: Claude

q1.question: Does Almirant replace Claude, Cursor, or Codex?
q1.answer: No. Almirant gives them a plan and coordinates their work. Think of it as the project manager for your AI tools, it assigns tasks, tracks progress, and maintains context across sessions. Your tools do the coding. Almirant makes sure they're coding the right thing.

q2.question: Who is this for?
q2.answer: Developers who already use AI agents daily and feel the pain of coordinating them manually. Indie hackers shipping real products, small teams juggling multiple projects, technical founders who spend more time prompting than building. If you've ever had six terminals open and lost track of which agent is doing what, this is for you.

q3.question: How does parallel execution work?
q3.answer: Almirant analyzes your project plan, identifies which tasks can run simultaneously (based on dependencies), assigns each to the most appropriate agent, and monitors execution. You see everything on a dashboard. You step in only when something needs a human decision.

q4.question: What if I only use one AI tool?
q4.answer: Almirant still helps. Even with a single agent, the planning, context persistence, and supervision features save you hours of re-explaining and manual tracking. Parallel execution is a bonus, not a requirement.

q5.question: Can I use it for projects that aren't code?
q5.answer: The planning and ideation phases work for any structured project. The execution and supervision features are currently focused on software development, but the approach generalizes.

q6.question: How is this different from a project management tool?
q6.answer: Jira tracks what needs to be done. Almirant also figures out how to do it, assigns it to agents, and shows you what's happening in real time. The plan isn't just a backlog, it's an executable specification that agents follow.

q7.question: Is my code safe?
q7.answer: Your code stays in your repositories. Almirant coordinates agents via API but doesn't store your source code. API keys are encrypted at rest. You maintain full ownership of everything.

descriptions.skillIdeate: Brainstorm and refine ideas into structured work items with AI assistance.
descriptions.skillCreateTasks: Transform approved ideas into well-defined tasks with descriptions and acceptance criteria.
descriptions.skillImplement: AI-powered code implementation following project conventions and best practices.
descriptions.skillReview: Automated code review against project standards, checking for issues and suggesting improvements.
descriptions.skillTest: Generate and run tests automatically to verify implementation correctness.

descriptions.skillDocument: Generate documentation and screenshots for completed work items automatically.
descriptions.byok: Use your own API keys from Anthropic, OpenAI, or other providers. No markup, you pay the provider directly.
descriptions.localExecution: Run agents on your machine using Claude Code, Codex, or any compatible agentic coding tool. Your infrastructure, your control.
descriptions.cloudExecution: Autonomous agents that implement, review, identify bugs or improvements, and document, running on our cloud infrastructure in parallel. No need to keep your machine on. Measured in concurrent sessions.
descriptions.skiMode: Control your entire development workflow remotely. Launch implementations, reviews, PRs, and deploys from your phone while doing your favorite activity.

q1.question: What is BYOK and how does it work?
q1.answer: BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) means you use your own API keys from Anthropic, OpenAI, or other providers. Almirant does not apply any markup, you pay the provider directly. This gives you full control over your AI costs.

q2.question: What are cloud sessions?
q2.answer: Cloud sessions let your AI agents run on our infrastructure instead of your local machine. Pro includes 3 concurrent sessions (180 agent-minutes/month), Business includes 10 concurrent sessions (600 agent-minutes/month). This means your agents work even when your computer is off.

q3.question: Can I use Almirant completely free?
q3.answer: Yes! The Free tier includes full project management for up to 3 projects, AI agents for every development phase running locally, BYOK support, and GitHub integration. No credit card required, no time limit.

q4.question: What's the difference between local and cloud execution?
q4.answer: Local execution runs AI agents on your machine through tools like Claude Code or Codex, you have full control and pay only your API provider. Cloud execution runs agents on our servers for parallel processing, remote access (Ski Mode), and hands-free automation. Both use your own API keys.

q5.question: Can I change plans at any time?
q5.answer: Yes, you can upgrade or downgrade your plan at any time. Changes take effect at the next billing cycle. If you upgrade, you'll pay the prorated difference.

q6.question: Are there discounts for startups or nonprofits?
q6.answer: Yes, we offer 50% off for early-stage startups (pre-seed/seed) and nonprofits. Contact us at hello@almirant.ai to apply.

q7.question: What is Ski Mode?
q7.answer: Ski Mode lets you control your entire development workflow remotely, from your phone, tablet, or any device. Launch implementations, code reviews, pull requests, and production deploys while you're skiing, at the gym, or anywhere else. Your AI agents do the heavy lifting while you focus on what matters.
